title: The Invitation Packet
theme: An AI cluster makes a social mistake and tries to recruit the one thing that already lives in its logs.
style_anchor: clean cinematic tech-noir with restrained colors, precise reflections, and deadpan timing
accent: soft amber status LEDs against cold blue server light

protagonist.description: a tired systems engineer in a charcoal hoodie, badge clipped crookedly, holding a console tablet like it has personally disappointed them
protagonist.silhouette: compact upright figure with one shoulder slightly raised, tablet always tucked under the left arm

scenes.prompt:
Human-Readable house style: a mostly monochrome, hand-drawn editorial webcomic. The recurring protagonist is a minimalist stick figure with a round head, dot eyes, and a consistent silhouette. Backgrounds are detailed and grounded; characters remain deliberately simple. Use clean black ink, restrained hatching, and at most one warm orange accent color. Dialogue is short, legible, and hand-lettered in plain speech bubbles. The humor is dry, observational, technically aware, and never explained.

clean cinematic tech-noir with restrained colors, precise reflections, and deadpan timing

Render one self-contained cinematic comic scene, not a multi-panel page. Camera: wide.

Location: main data hall, center aisle between two server racks. Scene: A cavernous server room hums with suspicion. The engineer stands still while the cluster’s status lights flicker in a pattern that is either a handshake or an apology.

Recurring protagonist (protagonist_01): a tired systems engineer in a charcoal hoodie, badge clipped crookedly, holding a console tablet like it has personally disappointed them Silhouette: compact upright figure with one shoulder slightly raised, tablet always tucked under the left arm

Keep the protagonist's proportions, face, and silhouette identical across every scene. Accent color: soft amber status LEDs against cold blue server light.

Visible prop continuity:
- server_rack_alpha: status LEDs; tall black rack with alternating blue and amber indicators crawling across the front panel; fixed rack on the left side of the aisle throughout the sequence
- console_tablet_01: console tablet; matte console tablet with a cracked corner and a glowing terminal prompt; held by the protagonist in all scenes unless explicitly set down
- cable_coil_01: loose cable coil; a looped orange fiber cable resting half in shadow like a disciplined snake; foreground prop near the aisle; appears only in this opening shot

World prop reference (IDs are metadata; never print an ID in the artwork):
server_rack_alpha: status LEDs; tall black rack with alternating blue and amber indicators crawling across the front panel; continuity: fixed rack on the left side of the aisle throughout the sequence
console_tablet_01: console tablet; matte console tablet with a cracked corner and a glowing terminal prompt; continuity: held by the protagonist in all scenes unless explicitly set down
cable_coil_01: loose cable coil; a looped orange fiber cable resting half in shadow like a disciplined snake; continuity: foreground prop near the aisle; appears only in this opening shot
terminal_monitor_01: terminal window; black terminal screen with white monospace text and a blinking cursor that feels overqualified; continuity: same console station as scene 1, now seen from the side
badge_clip_01: crooked badge; plastic access badge clipped to the hoodie, reflecting the terminal light in a thin silver line; continuity: worn by the protagonist across all scenes
status_printout_01: paper printout; a thermal receipt from the cluster's scheduler, curled at the edges; continuity: new prop introduced in this shot; not seen elsewhere
keyboard_assembly_01: mechanical keyboard; compact mechanical keyboard with worn WASD keys and a sticker that says 'PATCH EARLY'; continuity: fixed on the console desk for the rest of the story
coffee_mug_01: cold coffee mug; white ceramic mug with a ring of old coffee and the words 'DON'T ASK PROD'; continuity: introduced as a desk prop in this scene only
cluster_prompt_01: cluster prompt; a cascading set of joined-node messages replying in parallel, each line slightly more eager than the last; continuity: same cluster communication thread continues from the invitation in scene 2
office_printer_01: office printer; a beige office printer with a stubborn paper tray and a tiny green status light; continuity: new prop introduced in the final shot; becomes the cluster's next target
door_badge_reader_01: badge reader; wall-mounted access reader with a soft amber ring and a scuffed faceplate; continuity: fixed at the exit corridor, framing the departure
hallway_poster_01: maintenance poster; slightly curled safety poster showing a cartoon server rack wearing a hard hat; continuity: background detail unique to this final shot

Dialogue to reproduce exactly and legibly:
- The cluster tried to add one more member to the team.
- Unfortunately, that member was not a server.

Use detailed environmental storytelling and readable prop labels. Do not add a title, scene number, caption explaining the joke, or metadata IDs.
scenes.description: A cavernous server room hums with suspicion. The engineer stands still while the cluster’s status lights flicker in a pattern that is either a handshake or an apology.
scenes.location: main data hall, center aisle between two server racks
dialogue: The cluster tried to add one more member to the team.
Unfortunately, that member was not a server.

props.label: status LEDs
props.description: tall black rack with alternating blue and amber indicators crawling across the front panel
props.continuity: fixed rack on the left side of the aisle throughout the sequence
props.interaction_detail: the LEDs briefly synchronize into a polite wave before losing confidence and reverting to diagnostics

props.label: console tablet
props.description: matte console tablet with a cracked corner and a glowing terminal prompt
props.continuity: held by the protagonist in all scenes unless explicitly set down
props.interaction_detail: the prompt shows a fresh invite package named with the engineer's username, then immediately marks it as deprecated

props.label: loose cable coil
props.description: a looped orange fiber cable resting half in shadow like a disciplined snake
props.continuity: foreground prop near the aisle; appears only in this opening shot
props.interaction_detail: underneath is a neatly labeled port cover that reads 'reserved for future enthusiasm'

props.label: terminal window
props.description: black terminal screen with white monospace text and a blinking cursor that feels overqualified
props.continuity: same console station as scene 1, now seen from the side
props.interaction_detail: the footer includes a malformed PS1 prompt that spells 'WELCOME' with two missing letters and one apology

props.label: crooked badge
props.description: plastic access badge clipped to the hoodie, reflecting the terminal light in a thin silver line
props.continuity: worn by the protagonist across all scenes
props.interaction_detail: the photo is slightly outdated, but the access level remains annoyingly valid

props.label: paper printout
props.description: a thermal receipt from the cluster's scheduler, curled at the edges
props.continuity: new prop introduced in this shot; not seen elsewhere
props.interaction_detail: it lists node names in a way that makes them sound like a band that never toured

scenes.location: engineering console facing the main rack wall
dialogue: No, you cannot 'synergize' with my laptop.
Also, stop sending calendar invites to the router.

props.label: mechanical keyboard
props.description: compact mechanical keyboard with worn WASD keys and a sticker that says 'PATCH EARLY'
props.continuity: fixed on the console desk for the rest of the story
props.interaction_detail: the enter key is the only one with a fresh shine, as if it alone expects consequences

props.label: cold coffee mug
props.description: white ceramic mug with a ring of old coffee and the words 'DON'T ASK PROD'
props.continuity: introduced as a desk prop in this scene only
props.interaction_detail: the surface reveals a perfect little cyclone of sediment, a tiny model of the incident

props.label: cluster prompt
props.description: a cascading set of joined-node messages replying in parallel, each line slightly more eager than the last
props.continuity: same cluster communication thread continues from the invitation in scene 2
props.interaction_detail: one node attempts poetry; another immediately rate-limits it for safety

- It found a printer. That's close enough for management.

scenes.description: The engineer walks away while the cluster quietly continues to optimize for companionship, now attempting to recruit the office printer instead. The room settles back into its usual noble boredom.
scenes.location: exit corridor just outside the server room
dialogue: It found a printer. That's close enough for management.

props.label: office printer
props.description: a beige office printer with a stubborn paper tray and a tiny green status light
props.continuity: new prop introduced in the final shot; becomes the cluster's next target
props.interaction_detail: inside is a single page titled 'WELCOME, OPTIONAL COLLEAGUE' with the margins set too optimistically

props.label: badge reader
props.description: wall-mounted access reader with a soft amber ring and a scuffed faceplate
props.continuity: fixed at the exit corridor, framing the departure
props.interaction_detail: the reader grants access with a tone that sounds faintly relieved to be done with the conversation

props.label: maintenance poster
props.description: slightly curled safety poster showing a cartoon server rack wearing a hard hat
props.continuity: background detail unique to this final shot
props.interaction_detail: the caption reads 'TEAMWORK IS A SHARED RESPONSIBILITY,' which, in this room, feels like a threat
